content.problemStatement:
You are given a rooted tree with NNN nodes (numbered 111 through NNN). Node 111 is the root. You are also given integer sequences x1,x2,…,xNx_1, x_2, \ldots, x_Nx1​,x2​,…,xN​ and v1,v2,…,vNv_1, v_2, \ldots, v_Nv1​,v2​,…,vN​.

Let SSS be a subset of nodes. It is called valid if it is non-empty and the following conditions hold:


There is no pair of nodes (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that i,j∈Si,j \in Si,j∈S and iii is an ancestor of jjj.
The greatest common divisor of the values xix_ixi​ for all nodes i∈Si \in Si∈S (let's denote it by GGG) is greater than 111.
Next, let's define the value of SSS as G⋅VG \cdot VG⋅V, where GGG is defined above and V=∑i∈SviV = \sum_{i \in S}{v_i}V=∑i∈S​vi​.

You need to find a valid subset of nodes with the maximum value.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
1≤xi,vi≤100,0001 \le x_i, v_i \le 100,0001≤xi​,vi​≤100,000 for each valid iii
at least one valid subset exists
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 1,000,0001,000,0001,000,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that nodes uuu and vvv are connected by an edge.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi​ and viv_ivi​.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, print three lines.


The first of these lines should contain two space-separated integers G⋅VG \cdot VG⋅V and GGG for your chosen subset.
The second line should contain a single integer KKK ― the size of your subset.
The third line should contain KKK space-separated integers ― the numbers of the nodes in this subset (in any order).
If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
